_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning about different measures of spread and how useful each one is for different distributions.

_source.w: We are learning about different measures of spread and how useful each one is for different distributions.
y: Different measures of spread (range, IQR, standard deviation) are suited to different situations — choosing wisely is a key statistical skill.
Understanding the strengths and limitations of each measure prevents misleading conclusions.
This knowledge is essential for interpreting research data, financial reports and quality control statistics.
eg: Comparing range (sensitive to outliers) with IQR (resistant to outliers) for a dataset with extreme values.
Explaining why standard deviation is preferred for approximately normal distributions.
Choosing IQR and median to describe a heavily skewed distribution.
Calculating and comparing all three measures of spread for a given dataset.
Discussing: 'A report uses range to describe salary spread, but IQR would be more informative. Why?'
Using measures of spread to compare the consistency of two manufacturing processes.
